name: Luna 24
first: Last Soviet lunar mission; last uncrewed lunar landing for 37 years
description: The Soviet Union's final lunar mission and the last uncrewed lunar landing until China's Chang'e 3 in 2013 — a gap of 37 years. The drilled core extracted regolith from 2 m depth and returned 170 g to Earth. Among the samples: minerals containing trace water, foreshadowing the discovery of substantial lunar water ice deposits 30 years later. Luna 24 also marked the closure of the Soviet lunar programme; resources shifted to Mars and Venus.

events.label: DRILLING
events.note: Core sample extracted from 2 m depth.

events.label: ASCENT
events.note: Ascent stage lifts off; trans-Earth injection.

events.label: EARTH RETURN
events.note: Capsule lands in Surgut, USSR 1976-08-22 with 170 g of regolith.
